dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The incense and the smoke and stuff from that Altar of Ashes over there, it's energized the Shades about this place! And me, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to hear it, Commander. Even a brief visit to the Altar on a given night helps make me feel more prepared.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Keep doing what you're doing over there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Something transpired with the Silver Puddle back there, I could tell, young one! Your doing, or perchance that of the Fates themselves?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've revealed in the Pool the Aspects of the Nocturnal Arms. Not their true forms, exactly... but different ones, with their own great power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, the Nocturnal Arms! Your brother, he once used their counterparts, and those ones too turned into other stuff. Well if you need to test your newfound strength, I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The monstrous power of the Moonstone Axe is yours! There isn't much out there it couldn't chop clean through, including me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: While I do not wish to insinuate you're some kind of slacker, nonetheless I can't help but observe it's been more than a few nights since last you vanquished me, young one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought my training here was more or less complete, Commander. All the Nocturnal Arms are at my disposal, and I know them well. Am I to keep reducing you to dust?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, the choice is ultimately yours, of course, but lest you forget: There's always more for you to learn, and only one way to learn it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: We all saw you disappear into that Pitch-Black Stone. Another means by which you may spring into action, retaining the element of surprise! Pretty nice!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That Stone has been here all this time. It's so much more than a monument to Night, and its origins, even more ancient. But I never considered it a weapon in our arsenal...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, not like you can crack me over the head with it, but yeah!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples, they were given quite a fright when that Pitch-Black Stone there suddenly lit up. You know what it is, do you not?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A shard of purest darkness from the depths of Chaos... in its countless facets, countless outcomes. Perhaps none of which the Fates have preordained.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You keep on vanishing into the Pitch-Black Stone, young one. My disciples and I, we've been thinking must be something pretty good in there!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well Commander, it's not exactly a feast on the other side. Whenever I use the Stone, in many ways it's just like any other night, except... I really shouldn't say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw c'mon why not? Not like somebody's gonna tell on you, I mean, have we not earned your trust, or what?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: The Stone reveals its own secrets, not I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Something's changed about that Altar of yours recently, and you have been at it more and more, young one! Mayhaps awakened newfound power there, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've consecrated the Ashes and gained insight into the potential of the Arcana, Commander. Requires a little Moon Dust, and a lot of focus... perhaps the other way around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Moon Dust? Think the old Broker started pawning off that stuff, though I have heard it's possible a witch like you could brew it up herself. Sounds like you better stock up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How is the morale amongst all your disciples recently, Commander? Prometheus and Typhon present imposing threats that may shake even the greatest resolve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh they're fine! Just look at 'em, brave as you're gonna get. No matter what catastrophe should come to pass, it's just another night to us here!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're trying to prevent catastrophe, not let it become the norm. Well, please let me know if anything changes. We need you all at your best.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Hear that, you slackers? Better bring your best, or else!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander. You once held a place of honor in my father's House, so you must know Cerberus? I think I found him in the Mourning Fields, coated in Miasma, heads to paws. Was he always prone to uncontrolled fits of rage?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, he mostly sat around. We go way back, it's just... I kept strategically afar, seeing as he's kind of like a dog, and I'm kind of like a bone. So now he's giving you some trouble, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's not his fault. Chronos must have cast him from the House and put him out to pasture. But if I can just break through all that grime, I'm certain I can calm him down.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: He never did like baths, just so you know...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, you once held a place of honor in my father's House. So, you must know Cerberus, right? I found him in the Mourning Fields, coated in Miasma, heads to paws. Was he always prone to uncontrolled fits of rage?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, he mostly sat around. We go way back, it's just... I kept strategically afar, seeing as he's kind of like a dog, and I'm kind of like a bone. So now he's giving you some trouble, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's not his fault. Chronos must have cast him from the House, put him out to pasture. I broke through all the grime, and calmed him down. But he may need another cleansing soon.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: He never did like baths, just so you know...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So how's it going with that giant mutt? Still up to no good in the Mourning Fields? He's probably just bored.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've been able to break him free of whatever's taken hold of him there, though the affliction seems to keep coming back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That figures. He was the dreaded Guardian of the Gates! What, you think he'd let you pass just the one time, then never try to block you again?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I was hoping he would, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you seen, Commander? Lord Charon's come to visit us! At last our supply lines are secure, and our counter-assault begins.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh, yeah! It's been quite some time since good Lord Charon visited us last. We'd better all be on our best behavior around him! But, uh... where'd you get all that supply-lines stuff?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Why, he told me himself, sir! In his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, young one? I'd ask of you a question that pertains to good Lord Charon, if you please. Seems like you understand him pretty good! Has he, uh, said anything of late regarding me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, I... I'm sorry, no. But I'll be certain to put in a good word! You've been indispensable, and Lord Charon ought to be aware.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: No! I mean, um, no, just... don't go doing anything like that! You need not burden more the Boatman with such trivialities, amid such turbulent times...! Don't bother him, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You're eager to get out there and start dishing out some good old fashioned punishment, young one! Well then you need not stop for me. Just go!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It doesn't feel right to rush through these Crossroads without so much as a word. Besides, you surely have some vital wisdom to impart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That I do, and it is this: Even if you see a sign over my head or anybody's that's like c'mere talk to me, remember... no need to chat if you're not in a conversating mood, OK?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Just ignore anybody I don't want to talk to, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples indicate you reached your destination but you choked a little right there at the end. Well, that's rough. To have come that far only to wind up all the way back here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, this is not the type of encouragement that's of benefit right now. I had Chronos cornered, but he overpowered me. And now I've lost the element of surprise!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, yeah, that's bad. Look, I hate to say this but I'd probably hang it up if I was you. Blowin' a chance like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, by the way, I meant to say nice going, on occasion of achieving your objective and all that! You did achieve it, right...? Showed Chronos the what-for?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was able to defeat him, yes, Commander. But, my ultimate objective is far from complete.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well, that's objectives for you! Soon as one's complete, along comes another... but you are gonna keep knocking 'em dead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Word reached me that this past night, you achieved what you set out to do, young one! Major congrats on that, all my disciples and myself salute you on a job well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Commander! Though, this was a step toward victory I never could have achieved if not for all the training and counsel from Headmistress, Odysseus, and yourself.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: And she even gives credit where credit is due!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So you smacked Chronos good a couple of times now! Remind these Shades and me what is to happen next: How are you gonna kill that which continues coming back?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, though it's tempting to share all my latest progress, we both know I'm never to divulge the details of my task. Not even to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Not even to me. But can you say at least, your brother, is he gonna be all right? Been worrying about the guy is all...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples are appreciative for all of the improvements to these training grounds, young one! Soon we shall have a proper war camp here!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Anything we can do to raise morale is worth our while. Are there other improvements that you'd like to see?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah well, as you know I have but modest needs! A good smack in the head now and again is all that I require.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Just let me know if there's anything else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, to have felt the deadly sting of the Sister Blades! Mayhaps these are the sharpest blades with which I've ever been sliced!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You see that thing we got in here? It's a Dream Comforter! Warm, soft, top-of-the-line materials... soon as you lie down, boom, you'll pass right out. Or so I've heard.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never thought of our training grounds as being suitable for relaxation... though, I suppose a calm and steady mind is just as vital as physical preparedness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That's right! So if you want to doze off, I say go for it, you earned it! My disciples and I would be honored to stand watch. It's what we do anyway!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So how's that Dream Comforter been working out, living up to its name or what? Ah, if only it had not been placed so cruelly out of reach. What am I, Tantalus over here?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's more than just a plush blanket; it's a gateway into a dream, from Lord Hypnos himself. Would you like to try it? I could try dragging you over, or ask Nemesis to carry you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, I'll not give in to such temptations, young one! I would never under any circumstances abandon my post! But if I were you, I would be lights-out over there right now.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Well let me know if you change your mind...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've always wondered something, Commander. Where do you go when you're not here? I mean, sometimes you only arrive after I switch between Nocturnal Arms...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That isn't anybody's business but mine own, young one! Do we not, each of us, require solitude? A locale where we might collect our bearings and our thoughts?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course, sir... I apologize for overstepping. We are the Unseen; we dwell in obscurity and guard our secrets.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: And we don't go pryin' in each other's personal affairs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Think back to when this all began, young one. By your own estimation, how many times do you suppose you've vanquished me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't know... I distinctly remember nineteen such occasions, but there could certainly have been more. Why?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Cause I'm trying to get somewhere, that's why. If you vanquish me a certain number of times... well let's just say it'll be good for my career.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You need but say how many times you must be slain.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: I would if I could but I can't. Just get me now and then, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You did it, by the way, kiddo! Any idea how many times you killed me now? Think big.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well if you mean from the very start, it must have been a thousand times at least since I was little. I've been striking you since I could barely hold a blade...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I just meant more recently, where it was closer to thirty-five, but I liked the gusto with which you responded, young one!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said that if you reached some sort of death-threshold, it would be beneficial to your military service?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Yeah I get a special discount now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey, young one, just minding my own business over here... for what knowledge could I possibly impart to she who vanquished the Titan of Time like it was nothin'?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, I merely wished to thank you. Were it not for all your teachings in the past, I wouldn't have prevailed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes! I almost forgot my teachings in the past! In turn, I congratulate you on achieving that which I wagered was impossible to my disciples here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: One must at times hold fast to one's emotions, and at others, know to let 'em loose. And when you want to let 'em loose, or try out one of them Nocturnal Arms, know that I'm here.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: This anger is for Chronos, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Way to go with that Tablet of Peace, young one! Our ranks are beginning to swell with Shades who may yet find great purpose in their immortal coil!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Their lives, such as they are, will only improve once my father is restored to his rightful throne.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Oh there's no doubt of that! But first, we fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You have an Animal Familiar now! Well, your disciples are my disciples, young one. They shall be trained and cared for here while you're away, and they can fight me also if they like?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The old storage case... so, it's an heirloom from my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That it is, and for as long as I can recollect. Look upon it knowing that the burden which you bear, you need not bear alone. Plus it'll keep your Keepsakes all in one handy spot!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A symbol of the gift-giving custom of Olympus, then. A tradition that now falls to us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You're beginning to fill out that Keepsake storage case, young one. Fine work, yet I must caution you not to collect just for collecting's sake...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The act of giving is its own intangible reward. Nectar sours when it's offered with impure intent.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Won't have impure intent on my damn training grounds.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Your struggles never are in vain, young one! You're no mere mortal; with each passing night, you grow in knowledge and in strength! 'Specially if you throw on that God Mode, then there won't be any stopping you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would that godhood was enough, Commander. Though, the last thing you said... erm, what exactly did you mean?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, God Mode? Well, it's this mode, you see. One which you're able to throw on! Or to throw off, entirely at whim! It's just an option's all I'm trying to say, so use it if you please!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I saw my father recently, Commander... bound in chains in deepest Tartarus, a prisoner of Time. After all he's been through, though... he couldn't even remember you by name!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: How, uh, how terrible that is, young one! Your papa and I, we couldn't have been closer. Perchance he merely has forgotten 'cause of everything he's going through.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe he'd just entirely forget one of his most trusted advisors. There must be some way to help him recall.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: No! You gotta let him come around on his own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, curse these brittle old bones, that they cannot withstand as much punishment from you as once they did! Used to be I could really take a hit, but lately? I don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, what has gotten into you? I thought you longed to be struck by whichever armament I had available, the more savagely the better. Should I be holding back?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Never! I long merely to absorb more punishment ere I fall to pieces and have to put myself back together. You are way stronger now, but I'm not! Just doesn't seem right! But there is something you can do...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: What's this? An incantation to augment bone density?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Just think about it, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, the Augmentation of Bone Density you taught me... it's a costly incantation. Are you certain you're prepared? If we proceed, there's no undoing the effect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Young one, I was born prepared. Put yourself in my place: Wouldn't you want to be able to take a real beatin'?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I prefer to avoid beatings altogether, but can see the appeal from your perspective. Your bones would become much more durable... as long as falling to pieces is not the best part for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: It's really not, because when I'm in pieces, I'm not getting hit, and that's the good stuff, let me tell you. So you gonna cast this thing or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahaha, fools! I am now entirely invincible, and soon? Real soon, you all shall dearly pay for that which you've said behind my back... vengeance shall finally be mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, what is the meaning of this? You're not yourself! I merely cast the Augmentation of Bone Density!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: And you played right into my fingerbones. For now I cannot possibly be slain! Just you try it and see what occurs!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I hope you learned your lesson, kiddo! You thought I had become more powerful than any foe you'd ever faced, only to discover your Augmentation of Bone Density did not do all that much...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, I feared the worst when you acted as though were, well, drunk with newfound power. I'd never seen that side of you before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor shall you see that side of me again! Provided you don't make a habit of casting incantations you get from strangers!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you're not a stranger. You're a trusted ally! Aren't you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, yeah, but... look forget it, all right? Just hit me like you used to, I don't even care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a relief you're not a vengeful adversary who deceived me into casting a spell that awakened his true and terrifying might, Commander, but... do you feel any different after the Augmentation of Bone Density, or not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, I do feel somewhat tougher than before, young one, so thanks! That's really all I figured your incantation was gonna do, but I had you going, didn't I?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I suppose, but... mostly you just made me realize that I'd be very sad if some evening you did not come back. You're not just our Commander... you're my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you really mean that, kiddo? 'Cause let's face it, sometimes all I do is stand here. And not even sometimes!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Of course I mean it, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So you've awakened newfound power in those Blades of yours. I figured they could pull a fancy trick like that! Your brother, his old weapons all worked pretty much the same!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Infernal Arms had hidden Aspects, too... though whose, I wonder. Perhaps one of your own, Commander! The Fates bestowed their weapons to heroes, as well as gods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Careful there, young one! The secrets of the Arms are never to be loudly blabbed about...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you ever miss it, Commander? Your time in service to the House of Hades. Training my brother. Holding a position of high honor there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, 'twas a glorious time for sure. But, I would not have earned my post if I kept crying about the past like a little baby. I'm all about the here-and-now!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But all of what you used to have is gone. The other servants of the House... you must have some concern for them at least?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: C'mon, they're gods! They'll probably be fine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that the enchanted Tooth I proffered you has proved invaluable? Although, since you're already back, perhaps it wasn't quite invaluable enough...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd surely not have gone as far without your Keepsake, Commander. Though, why is it known as the Luckier Tooth? Was there another of its kind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no! It is truly a unique artifact procured in my travails. An artifact with which you'll cling to life as might a sailor on a sinking ship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I must salute the bearer of the Argent Skull! Would that my own cranium were thusly reinforced with silver and could explode with such spectacular results!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, have you by any chance seen Nemesis making her way toward the gateway exits recently?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Nope! My head is squarely turned the other way. Seen naught of anybody here besides yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well then you must have heard her heavy footfall at least. She's not exactly known for subtlety.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Can't say she is, but can't say I have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Must think you're pretty tough by now, don't you, beating Chronos or whoever down again and again! But are you pushing yourself to the fullest? Or taking it easy?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Few would consider the task of vanquishing the Titan of Time to be an easy one, Commander. Though I take your meaning. The Oath of the Unseen beckons...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, use it, my disciples and I are practically daring you! You'll get to spruce up your Nocturnal Arms, we'll get to see what happens! Win-win!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you ventured to the bottom of the sea. I, too, have been there in those depths, young one! But once, awhile back, and briefly, I might add!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course...! You were a famous mariner in your mortal days! But, then you finally met your end beneath the waters upon which you sailed?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Nah! 'Twas way more glorious than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: How have you lately fared against the Lurkers and the Serpents all that way down in the watery depths? Knowing of 'em and squaring off against 'em are two very different things.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're significantly stronger than the Lost Souls lingering around here. And they have no sense of fear at all, attacking without any hesitation.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: They're basically just walking fish, young one. Not much for conversating, but they'll stick you with a trident just fine! Don't you let 'em.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples and I all salute you on account of having made it up to the big mountain, young one! Back when we drew breath, we could only dream of those hallowed heights...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I appreciate the encouragement, Commander, but I've got a bit of a climb still. The legions of Chronos are there in droves... and the mountain's defenses can't tell us apart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely you jest! The mechanical marvels wrought by the gods themselves turned their deadly appendages on you? When you went all that way to help 'em out?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know I've always wondered about all those commendations that you wear... which was the first you earned? Which means the most?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you mean these little numbers here? One of these I've had since, um, the Siege of Ilium! Speared many a guy for that one! The others are from campaigns 'cross the land.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Why are you looking at me with that consternated face, young one? Mayhaps in search of wisdom you would like me to impart?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...It's just, even now, I know so little of your past. I have to admit, Commander... there are such times when I begin to feel as though you're not being candid with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What?! No. Look, I'm here to focus on your martial expertise, rather than prattle on about the way things used to be! But fine. What is it you really wish to know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I wish to know exactly who you truly are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well I'm not telling you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey look, I know we kinda had a little spat last time, and I don't want things going sideways with you here, so let me give it to you straight.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Very well, Commander. You always have my full attention.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The thing is, kiddo, and it's something that could get me into lots of trouble: Not only do I not want to, but I'm not even supposed to tell you what you want to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I suppose you can't tell me why not. Is somebody coercing you to keep me ignorant?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, just trust me on this one, OK? You've got your part to play, and I got mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, if I'm to trust you as I always have, then I need to know that you are not concealing anything from me. If you fear reprisal, I swear to vouch for you and protect you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My fear, young one, is that if I share the knowledge you desire, you shall never again look upon me in the same way! Which may not be so good for either one of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I would rather look upon you knowing you are honest with me. And for my part, I shall not betray your confidentiality.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_TrueMythologicalOrigin_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well all right... I am not exactly the Commander whom you take me for, nay! I was much more than that... King Schelememnon, I was called! But I was even more than that, as well! For your own papa hand-selected me as one of his Three Judges of the Dead!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, that's... that's incredible! So then... after the Underworld fell, you went into hiding here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I, uh, heheh. Yes, that is correct. And our enemies, they have pursued me ever since! No one must know my whereabouts! Not your father, your brother... nobody.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why can't my father or my brother know? Surely they'd want to hear you're safe!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: No! Leave the clandestine communications to me.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: ...I always thought my father did the judging on his own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You see the pattern clearly now, don't you? Six Nocturnal Arms, four Aspects of each, and two ways out of here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There are patterns everywhere, but... the ones you're referring to, I can't say I've observed. What does it signify?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What does it signify? Pff, come on, I'd say it's pretty obvious by now. You really need me to spell it out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You must think you're pretty tough by now, young one. Nocturnal Arms? Got 'em all. Monsters, Titans? No problem. But you're still missing something if you ask me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well then... what am I missing by your estimation, Commander? You likely know my areas of deficiency better than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What you're missing is the lifelong or rather deathlong devotion and respect from my disciples here. Oh they'll sort of smile to your face, but behind your back? I dunno...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? If they're being derisive then perhaps they are unfit to stand among our ranks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: No, wait! I just meant maybe you ought to spend more Kudos on this place. With that mouthy Shade over there? That ought to put everybody right in your pocket!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You did it, kiddo! My disciples, they are very much impressed not only with your battle-prowess but with all your hard-spent Kudos, which have made these Crossroads livelier, indeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to have had the opportunity. It's been tempting to focus on improvements by the cauldron, when these training grounds have needed something too.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Got to make a good impression on new recruits!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, what is the consensus here about Prometheus? If some mortals venerated him, perhaps you and your disciples feel conflicted that he now opposes us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan who brought fire, reason, good eats, and the arts and stuff to mortalkind... then faced the vengeance of the gods for all of that. Can't stand the guy! Let him have it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you can't stand him even though he did so much for you? Though mortals can be difficult to please...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well let's see, has he been nice to you, or has he tried to kill you? So what if he buttered us up way back when! Not like he's done much for us lately.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: He has tried to kill me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense a great weight bearing down on you, young one. Not quite as bad as what old Atlas got, but still!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What do you advise as my next move, Commander: to pursue our enemy in the Underworld, or come to the aid of Olympus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well not to be all greedy here, but... I say go wherever you can get the stuff you need the most, right now! We got some stuff down here they don't got up there, and so on, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well we did it, kiddo! Or at least I did. This whole experience... it has given me a lot to think about.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: At least you should have time to think about it now, Commander. Tell me if you have any epiphanies?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You bet. But I will probably just not have much to say, I mean, I'm beat! Not beat enough by you, of course, can't ever get enough of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Your eyes do not deceive! Near the gateways outta here you'll find none other than the Oath of the Unseen, I think it's called. Ask me not whence it came... instead ask just how tough you really are!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I earned the right to look upon the Oath... the Testaments of Night. Do I dare speak the vows? And thereby spread the Fear that darkness brings...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, what are you, scared? Those Nocturnal Arms you got, they probably are gonna love that Fear and Testament type stuff. I say give it a shot! What's the worst that could happen?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All mortalkind condemned to never-ending torment and despair...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: How goes it with the never-ending torment stuff, now that you've got that Oath thing over there? Those Nocturnal Arms of yours, they must be having a great time!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Arms of Night... they're reacting to the Oath, I think. Growing stronger as her Testaments are carried out. Drinking the very Nightmares born of mortals' fear of the dark.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Now that is what I like to hear!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even in all your wisdom, Commander... how is it that you know so much about the Oath of the Unseen? As far as I know it pre-dates even my father's rule within the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh yeah, your papa took a different tack. Was all about his Pacts of Punishment! He even served your brother with 'em once, when they were really on the outs. But boyo just went for it, and then things really heated up!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: And I'm not gonna! For I made certain Vows myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: We feared the worst on such a terrifying night. Not so easy out there with that Oath of the Unseen doing its thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Vows do make my task more difficult; but they make me grow stronger, little by little. Are you suggesting I let up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean I'm only saying if it's all too much you ought to turn that thing back down, nobody's judging over here! I'd rather you let up than give up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You getting a handle on that walking stick of yours? There's other stuff you might like better in the Puddle back there, if you've got the Silver for it. Just be sure to try it all out on me!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: You mustn't speak ill of Descura, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So this is the Black Coat! An armament such as the world has never before seen! Probably because that thing can punch or blow up anybody fool enough to cross your path!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: It fits like it was made for me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You're brave to venture through that previously-warded gateway, but come on! You Underworld types aren't cut out for surface life! Unless perchance there is an Incantation that could help...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think there is, Commander, except... it calls for reagents that can only be found up there... but, how am I supposed to gather them before the incantation is complete?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Mayhaps just make a run for it, I don't know. So what if you can't stay long, just grab what you need and get out of there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You remained upon the surface for considerably longer than before. How was it, any good?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: War rages above. The Olympians sent Heracles himself to contain the damage. Shades of the dead linger everywhere. So... not good, all in all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Shades of the dead! Sure sounds like more disciples we can use. Eh, sprint into 'em, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand your recent journey to the surface didn't last so long, young one. Life's hard up there, and always gonna be! Not like your cushy Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not accustomed to it yet. Everything's intensified... whenever I fail to evade a foe, I feel it even more.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Oh you're not gonna have an easy time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Your path is fraught, young one. Know that I'm always here to let you vent all your anger and frustrations upon, as well as to train with.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: The greatest teacher is out there...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What was that you were saying about your tenure just before, Commander?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Nothing. Just that I have it now. Because some of my disciples, especially some of the newer ones, they have made inquiries into the terms of my servitude...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No insubordination toward the Commander shall be tolerated, do you hear me, Shades?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Eh, hehehe. You guys all heard that right??

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: How fared your recent surface sea-journey, young one? I still remember well the boundless waters there. How blue and tranquil they were!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Tranquil? My experience was not exactly that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The Umbral Flames, young one! You're the spitting image of the big lady herself with those in hand. May they ever light your way, and light up anybody you don't like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Observe how patient my disciples are, studying your every move! If ever you're inclined to give them a demonstration of your prowess, or just want to blow off some steam, do not hesitate to whale upon me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember you are training with an unarmed opponent here, young one. Out there, the skeletons can move... and they'd cut you to ribbons, given half a chance! You won't learn to handle that kind of pressure sparring with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Your myriad accomplishments thus far are nothing in comparison to that which you now stand to gain, young one! Eternal glory can be yours, if you but prove your worth!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The patterns on that drapery... the secret Gifts of the Veil, bestowed on Night's most dedicated servants...! Commander, what are they doing here? Shades are never to look upon them!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well let's just say the rules have lately changed! Those Gifts, as you refer to them, are yours to earn... provided you can do the stuff the Oath of the Unseen would ask of you! Which, as you probably surmised, shall be no simple feat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps a test merely of greed and arrogance... to overextend with the Oath may put my task in jeopardy.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: C'mon, don't be such a baby!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So are you gonna earn the Gifts of the Veil over there? Or squander your chance to get some of that eternal glory?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eternal glory... if such a thing may come to those who manage to achieve extraordinary feats, so be it. But to chase glory as a goal unto itself? That can't be right... at least, it's never been our way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: But our ways can change, wouldn't you say? We mustn't become mired in the olden ways of doing things! Though I don't mean to pressure you, kiddo, it's just... I think you ought to get what's yours, is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To think the Gifts of the Veil were those statues... I always thought it was the Veils themselves... the draperies, sewn with the finest fabric of the Fates. Where are they anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, those? We, uh, we sent 'em back into the shadows whence they came. They were just packing materials, you know? You got the real deal, I swear!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I still cannot entirely believe that Night would honor me with such an ostentatious display. Although, perhaps these Gifts are less for me and more for those who serve our cause...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that you mention it, the Shades do like to gawk at 'em when you're not here. You get to inspire them even if you're not around! If that's not eternal glory... what is?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These Shades are inspired because I went out of my way to use the Oath of the Unseen purely for personal gain?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah! They're easy to please! But not you, apparently. I didn't know Night's Champion was gonna have so many questions all the time! You want the statues, or don't you?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Good! 'Cause we can't send 'em back now that you opened 'em up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Halfway to victory, kiddo! But to earn the Gifts of the Veil, one must conquer the Underworld and the surface realms upon a Fear-ful night, each in turn. You up for it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For the glory of the Underworld and the everlasting rule of Night, I would do everything in my ability. Whether that makes me worthy of the Gifts of the Veil, I know not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: C'mon, what kind of attitude is that? You did the Underworld already, now you just got to do the surface, and the Gifts are yours! First one of 'em at least. Come on, do it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Halfway to victory, young one! But to earn the Gifts of the Veil, one must conquer the Underworld and the surface realms upon a Fear-ful night each in turn. You up for it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For the glory of the Underworld and the everlasting rule of Night, I would do everything in my ability. Whether that makes me worthy of the Gifts of the Veil, I know not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: C'mon, what kind of attitude is that? You did the surface already, now you just got to do the Underworld, and the Gifts are yours! First one of 'em at least. Come on, do it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The second Gift of the Veil is almost within reach! Could actually just go right up and touch it, b-but anyway a-are you prepared to use the Oath and go the other way?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't mean to leave you in suspense, Commander, but we'll just have to see.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: I shall be waiting here with the equivalent of bated breath!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The second Gift of the Veil is almost within reach! Could actually just go right up and touch it, b-but anyway a-are you prepared to use the Oath and go the other way?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't mean to leave you in suspense, Commander, but we'll just have to see.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: I shall be waiting here with the equivalent of bated breath!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The final and most monumental Gift of the Veil is so close at hand! If you can get through one of your two routes under the Oath, then surely the rest is but a walk in the park!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can only assume a walk in this park you cite must be an absolutely grueling experience.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Some parts of town? Ohh yeah.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The final and most monumental Gift of the Veil is so close at hand! If you can get through one of your two routes under the Oath, then surely the rest is but a walk in the park!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can only assume a walk in this park you cite must be an absolutely grueling experience.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Some parts of town? Ohh yeah.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Mind telling all these Shades that no, the Father of All Monsters isn't back? Because they're so caught up with this outrageous rumor, they don't buy it when I tell 'em!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, the thing is... I confronted Typhon himself just last night. While Chronos schemes from the Underworld, that monstrosity attacks Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I, uh, I... knew all that, it's just... I was checking to see if my disciples had the nerve to stand up for that which they truly believed! And they did. Nice going, everyone!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: So you knew of Typhon's return?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, would you attest to my disciples that I've taught you a great deal when it comes to fighting Typhon? They've been getting a little unruly and, uh, you'd be doing me a favor!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But Commander, you've not told me anything about him... ah, I know what this is. You wish to see if I would stoop to speaking falsehoods merely because I was asked!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, yeah! Yeah that's right. And I'm asking real nicely, c—come on, young one, would you just do this for me here...?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I'll not bring shame upon us both.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, how am I to overcome a foe whose life-force is completely undetectable? Typhon the Bringer of Storms conceals his strength from me... but it must have its limits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, young one, most of us can't sense another's life-force quite like you. We just hit each other till somebody ends up dead! Maybe there's signs, like cuts and bruises and whatnot?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's not impervious. The gods have vanquished him before using sheer force...

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Just let him have it! Works with everybody else!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Settle a little wager here, young one. These Shades are making claims you felled the Father of All Monsters! I, however, am the voice of reason, and I say come onnn.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Typhon's recent defeat certainly wasn't my doing alone, Commander. We have Olympus on our side, and more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Right, um, yeah, that's what I said: Technically, it wasn't just you. Hear that, disciples?! She had a bunch of those fancy Boons and who only knows what else!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You have repeatedly knocked Typhon from his perch! Mayhaps the bigger they are... the harder they fall.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've never thought of it that way before, Commander, but you're right! The Cyclops Polyphemus also falls quite hard.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Because of his size, you see!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I try not to dwell on what shall happen if and when all this is finally over, Commander. I know I ought to be completely focused on the task, but... as we near a potential end, I cannot help but think ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well stop it! How many times have I told you, never think ahead, kiddo. No good is ever gonna come of that. The here-and-now is all that counts!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're truly saying one should never plan? But you're a Commander... anticipation and strategy are integral to martial leadership...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're always doing good right now, you're always doing good later, that's all there is to it! Now quit with your worryin'.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: There's something up with those Nocturnal Arms of yours... a certain aura that's surrounding one of them. The Grave Thirst...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A relatively simple incantation. I expected there was more to the connection between Night's weapons and the dead. No wonder you can see it, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, now... not like I need eyes on the back of my cranium! But I can feel it in my bones! Want more Bones yourself, just use the weapon with the Thirst!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander... there must be something you could tell me of my brother. The stronger my connection, the better our chances. You knew him, didn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew your brother, yeah. Good kid, most of the time. He wouldn't want you to be so hung up about him. What he'd want... more than anything, I think... is for you... to hit me. As hard as you can, repeatedly and often, and that's what I would want for you as well!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My brother must have been immensely strong, if he could sometimes even take my father when they sparred. Is there some favorite technique of his that you could pass along?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: No there is not! My disciples must each follow their own path. Keep following yours, and mayhaps you too shall become powerful enough to beat your papa up.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Commander! My father is bound in chains in Tartarus!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Then it ought to be easy!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Commander, we have no reason to fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples here claim that you clashed with none other than the Prince of the Underworld himself, and you fought with the fury of a daemon, to which I said, c'mon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's true, sir! I found myself in some competition-chamber in what looked to be Elysium. There Zagreus and I engaged in a full-contact battle for the hearts of our spectators!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that seems pretty pointless to me, kiddo, seeing as I trained you both! You are equally matched! I don't see what comparisons are even to be drawn!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, would you be willing to accompany me to the Springs sometime? We could rest our weary bones a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you gonna do, carry me? I'm required here, young one, for these Shades still require discipline! Mayhaps some other age, when all we hold dear isn't at stake...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Mayhaps you could give my disciples a little demonstration, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Less discussion, more concussion here, kiddo!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, young one, let's see you deliver your best shot already.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak not, but concentrate, and strike repeatedly at me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Patience, young one! You shall... probably get through this whole thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you come to train with me, perchance? Or merely passing through?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Less greeting, more beating, if you would be so kind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust you have some damage to impart?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Pray strike me down, young one, lest I be sad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold fast your questions and attack, young one!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us not conversate unnecessarily, though you can hit me if you want!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you not more important interactions to perform?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Each one of your attempts is but a step along your path. And so is hitting me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Pray focus on your path, or mayhaps beat me up a little first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My disciples all believe in you and stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Go forth, young one, and we shall keep vigil here ere you return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, these old bones are more resilient than they appear, see for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: There shall be time enough for idle chat when victory is ours!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak with your weapons, young one, not your words!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Things not going so well? Why not take it out on me?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak not to me, young one, but rather strike me down!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't you remind me what the Nocturnal Arms can do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: There is no such thing as too much practice, kiddo!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see your best moves, or worst moves, I don't even care!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Strike fast and true at me, or really in whichever manner you prefer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Less talking, more clocking, kiddo, give it to me here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on and give me your best shot! Just one, will ya?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Please don't go without at least a practice swing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I think that my disciples are due for a little demonstration!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Show to my disciples the height of your technique!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't keep your feelings bottled up, let me have 'em!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Long has it been since last you struck me down, young one...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I'm past due for a serious beating one of these nights.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Show us the peerless power of Night's Champion!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: If you dare go easy on me, I shall never forgive you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Less conversation, more... laceration, I don't know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something to say to me, let those Nocturnal Arms do the talking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure could go for some heavy bludgeoning right about now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I got a real hankering for some more cuts and slashes here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what sounds good is being burned or blasted to bits...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: A battle lost. But this is war, young one, and you have many battles yet to come! The only question is, are you prepared to win? I'm not so sure here, so let's see you give me twenty!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander Schelemeus, sir. You're certain it's acceptable for me to strike you twenty times in front of all your students?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Dead certain. Now hit me with everything you got!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, we've restored our fishing pier! Would you mind joining me there, given all your nautical experience?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My days amongst the fishes are long gone, young one. And besides, my place is here... right in this particular spot. Though, thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, though it falls to us to keep the old traditions alive, may you partake of this Nectar unburdened by the weight of its significance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm most appreciative of both the offering and of the sentiment. And I summarily shall do my part: Behold! The luckiest of my possessions is in your possession now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, the memories, young one... your brother thusly used to favor me. And for his sake and yours, I shall accept this. Now, please accept my gratitude: Hey, thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You are most welcome, Commander! And hey thanks to you in turn, for your peerless insight and steadfast presence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm most appreciative of your generosity. Would that I had more to give you in return besides the wisdom of my vast experience...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please, sir, that is more than enough. Your disciples and I are fortunate to study under you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I must not partake in such extravagances! For my disciples are counting on my clear-eyed counsel and undivided attention. But tell you what I'm gonna save this one for later when I'm off, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What you call an extravagance... it must be nothing compared to the luxuries of my father's House. Yet you never want for anything. Besides the occasional hit or two.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Many a time have I been regaled with the most exquisite beverages and snacks, but never before from such a disciplined and honorable disciple such as yourself! Rest of you slackers hear that? More offerings, come on!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Nectar's yours to do with as you please, sir! May it lighten the burden of responsibility you bear to all the Shades and others counting on your skill and knowledge.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Oh I'll give 'em skill and knowledge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Look kiddo... I got to level with you. Got kind of a drinking problem over here. Stuff goes right through me! I just don't want you wasting this when you could give it to one of those other guys out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, thank you for your honesty with me... I hate to think all that Nectar only served to make you ill-at-ease. You do know you don't need to drink it, right? It can be a collector's item... a reminder of your friends, and the affection that they have for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Affection, shmuffection, look I'm gonna keep it just this sixth time... but don't go giving any more to me, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you don't want Nectar at this point, but what if I were to offer you Ambrosia, sir? In fact it's not even a hypothetical, so here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well this is a most-handsome gift, and it has left me speechless, for I have not received such Kudos since your own brother gave to me a similar reward long ago...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? What was the circumstance then? Though this is no reward, merely a mark of our long-standing camaraderie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The circumstance was that the boyo had repeatedly made use of me for target practice at around that time, so the Ambrosia was in recompense.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've served as target practice for me far more times than I can easily recall, sir. So I wish for you to have this Ambrosia as a token of my gratitude. No one else would be so willing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Damn right no one else would be so willing! I've been in this profession longer than anybody, you've never seen me so much as move a muscle, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well you have no muscle, as far as I can see, Commander. But I know you've long served my family with honor, and such loyalty is never to be taken for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll tell you what, not everybody thinks like you, kiddo. A lot of guys I've met, they pay you for a job, and that's the end of it, no loyalty, no long-term prospects, or stability...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To the honorable Commander Schelemeus, ward of the bloodline of Hades, the Fate-appointed regent of the Underworld: May this Ambrosia forever guarantee your service, for as long as you are willing to provide it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey wait did you just grant me tenure in the family's name, kiddo? Can you even do that, and also... why?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm Princess of the Underworld; I'm well within my right. And as for why, I said before that I do not take loyalty for granted. I wanted you to know I meant it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Look I didn't really think you were gonna replace me or anything, but... it means a lot you'd do all that for me. I know I helped teach you to hit real hard, kiddo... but I never thought you'd get me right in the heart...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for everything, Commander.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Just doing what they pay me for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Take heed whensoever you observe some bozo with what looks to be a yellow barrier around 'em! Ere you punch through their armored protection, they can punch right back!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've never been able to make an armored foe so much as flinch from one of my strikes.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: That's perfectly normal, happens to all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: With experience, one develops knacks for certain things, and one of my knacks is detecting Bones, all shapes and sizes and in varied quantities, and kiddo: You have got a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We're in the realm of the dead, Commander. Bones aren't difficult to come by, especially this close to the surface. Though... the Broker may have better use for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: That's basically what I was gonna say! Sure maybe you could use a couple for an incantation here or there but the old Broker's really gonna eat 'em up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: All your training's gonna be for naught if you don't use it in a fight. Say I'm chasing after you with a club and you want to get me to stay put. Let's see you cast a Circle of Binding over here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't forget to use that Cast of yours. Stops any bozos in their tracks! And if you give 'em the Omega version... wham!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: A Binding Circle has its many uses to be sure...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Choose wisely, young one, ere you set forth each night! There's your Arcana at the Altar, your choice of Nocturnal Arms, your Keepsake, your Animal Familiar, I mean dang...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a lot to consider when you put it that way, Commander. But my objective is always clear: Find the Titan, slay the Titan. Free my family, free Olympus.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Well that was like four different things right there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Some of those bozos whom you run into out there are pretty tough! But your fancy Omega Moves ought to cut right through 'em as might a hot knife through butter. Battle getting old? Press and hold.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Magick is to be used sparingly, in times of need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Being a shadowy denizen of the Underworld comes with certain perks, young one! Take, for instance, how when you Dash, nothing can touch you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We are the Unseen... at one with the darkness and the dead. I can evade anything, yes. But only for a moment, and even then... I can't prevail by dodging all night long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, I'd do it all the time if I was you! See the little trail when you Dash? Once it snaps back, you're good to go again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't lose heart just 'cause you're getting whacked, all right? Between your Altar and that Silver Puddle back there, you're going to get tougher and tougher, till you're the one doing all the whacking!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: You mean the Silver Pool, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: So you have reached the limits of your Grasp of the Arcana. Happens to everyone! Just need some more of that Psyche and you'll soon be grasping for more of them Cards you got!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My connection to the dead, my connection to the past... and the Altar is the conduit! Commander, are you able to draw from the Arcana yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well used to be I could draw pretty good, but eh, maybe not as good as the pictures on those Cards.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The craft you seek to master takes its toll. But not for long, your magick power's gonna come right back by the time you get to the next place you're going. So use it, no point bottling it up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Make sure you're nabbing all the Ashes you can find out there, kiddo. Then you can smudge 'em on your Altar to unlock those fancy Cards! You'll be brawnier in no time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Arcana reveal my fullest potential... from the Ashes of the past, a possibility for the future.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: A possibility to smack some bozos harder than before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What gives, young one, you feeling stuck? I mean you are stuck, but you're getting somewhere in the spiritual sense!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And where might that be, Commander...? I'm nowhere near my goal of vanquishing the Titan yet. I need to go farther.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Probably just need to Dash instead of getting hit so much, and get some more of those Arcana Cards, they'll toughen you right up! Each night is gonna go a little different, too.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: May this one go a little better.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: My days of running around taking stuff may be over, but yours are only beginning, kiddo! Keep your eyes peeled for stuff you can use out there, and be sure to pack the best Tool for the job.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: You used to run around and... take stuff?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: What ails you, young one? You have a look of consternation, which mayhaps my breadth of experience could alleviate. Go on, just spit it out!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Commander... I was just thinking about all the reagents I still need. Some grow in different regions, or require different tools... it's a lot to keep in mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: But you don't gotta keep it all in mind! That Purple Pack you carry all of your stuff in, somehow... don't you got all your notes in there too? So if you don't remember where to nab something, check there! In fact, why don't you do it now?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I do have notes on everything in there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Your connection to the realm means you must have a sense of what's ahead at every turn. Like a window to the future at each threshold! You might not know what all the little symbols mean but you'll figure 'em all out eventually.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Windows to the future... and at a branching path I simply choose whichever one looks best.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: I surely would if I were in your stead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Could always use some new disciples here. See any Shades of the dead out there just loitering around, remember your training and sprint right into them! They'll come around.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They'll more than come around, sir. They're prepared to join our fight. By now they know exactly who I am, and what we stand for here.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: May you keep running into lots of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand that Great Selene graced you with one of them Hexes that she's got. A powerful effect, but you must use a bunch of magick first to charge it up! Eh, like charging a crystal!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, I thought most of your experience was in the martial ways. But you know the intricacies of magick as well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: You have grown quick, young one. Real quick! Would that I, too, could dash so fast as to become untouchable, or sprint as fleetly as a... she-wolf, or something? Give me a couple of laps!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: All of those Nocturnal Arms of yours got their own different moves! Do not dwell upon which one of 'em is strongest; rather, choose whichever one really speaks to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And what if they all speak to me? Faintly, but still.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Then pick the one that gives you extra Bones!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Commander, what say all your disciples of the reappearance of the Fates? I take it you've already heard.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes, I've heard all right... Fates, Shmates is what they say, if you must know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fates, Shmates, what is that supposed to mean? It sounds rather disrespectful on initial impression.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Disrespectful? Oh far be that from me! I merely say Fates, Shmates and ask that you consider the weight of my words.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't care that a new age may dawn... that the rule of gods may end.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Kiddo, I'll believe it when it happens. We'll probably all be dead by then.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm immortal and the rest of you already are dead!

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Just sayin' don't hold your breath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: The rousing tales of your great accomplishment preceded you, young one! And I am here to heap still-further praise: Great job back there with all the Chronos stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you very much, Commander, though... what are you still doing here? I kept expecting you'd return to your honorary post at the House, but eventually I had to depart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Well you are not exactly done with everything, are you? I see you eyeballing the gateways out, wondering what's gonna happen now. So as long as you're here... so am I!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So then you're sworn to us... to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I meant to note... I had an unusual experience at the House of Hades. I heard no mention of you there, despite your distinguished status. Only of a skeleton who would stand watch outside... Schele... Schelae... perhaps akin to that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Wha?! Kiddo. You think we skeletons are all the same? That's way over the line, pal! Come on, I would have thought... Bwuh! Unbelievable!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, I... I didn't mean to suggest... eugh, I apologize.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Commander, is that supposed to be... me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: It is Night's Champion foretold by Fate! She who shall strike Fear into the hearts of those who stand against the realm and stuff like that! I can see you're at a loss for words, such is the splendor of her likeness! And this is just the first of 'em!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. And are the other Gifts just as, erm... detailed? Either way, I'm not so certain I can handle twice let alone three times the glory, when my task is difficult enough as it is...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, so then the Fear is getting to you. Do not give in to it, young one! Instead, give in to Curiosity about those other glorious Gifts! Think of the prestige! Now you can be a little scaredy quitter, or Night's Champion, which is it gonna be?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: ...Do I have to decide this instant?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Nah you take however long you need.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...Commander, I... I'm rather at a loss for words, I think! Though I am honored, truly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you better be! You got any idea what I — I mean the Fates must have gone through to put these things together? But Night's Champion deserves only the best... which is that last one you haven't seen just yet!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My mind reels at the thought of a sculpture in excess of those. It's covered in Night's drapery... what's to stop me from merely taking a look? Apparently you've already seen it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like you might not be Night's Champion after all, suggesting underhanded tactics such as that! You want to win top honors, you gotta have honor. And that means no peeking! Now you gonna use the Oath again, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I don't know. All this still strikes me as a bit unwise. While I value your encouragement and am inspired by your words of validation, perhaps I shouldn't be so quick to pin my sense of self-worth on accumulating material possessions.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
endLines.text: Come on, young one, you mustn't speak that way!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... I'm deeply honored, Commander. To be in service to the realm, and to have earned such... provocative sculptures. I thought art was about the expression of beauty; I never realized that it could inspire so many... complicated emotions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Skelly_01
dialogueLines.text: I got a lot of complicated emotions just thinking about what it took to get those things in here without you noticing! But anyway, congrats. We'd have a feast in your honor or something, but... eh, nobody's supposed to know about this stuff. So it's gonna have to be our secret and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So, does this mean I truly am Night's Champion? And eternal glory is mine?
